summary: The weekly review pulse highlights significant user experience issues, financial and transactional concerns, and product and interface improvement needs. 40% of users face user experience issues, 30% encounter financial and transactional concerns, and 30% suggest product and interface improvements.
themes: [{"name": "User Experience Issues", "percentage": 40}, {"name": "Financial and Transactional Concerns", "percentage": 30}, {"name": "Product and Interface Improvements", "percentage": 30}]
quotes: ["Worst experience. Very high transaction fees. Uninstalling right now.", "Interface is complex and not good. you cannot increase font size.", "INDmoney offers useful features, but constant UI/UX changes make it frustrating to use."]
action_items: ["Revamp the user interface to improve usability and consistency", "Address financial and transactional concerns by reducing fees and improving transaction processes", "Implement a feature to allow users to customize their homepage and prioritize user-friendly design"]
fee_scenarios: [{"type": "exit_load", "scenario_name": "Mutual Fund Exit Load", "explanation_bullets": ["A mutual fund exit load is a fee charged to investors when they redeem their units from a mutual fund scheme.", "The exit load is usually a percentage of the redemption amount and is deducted from the investor's redemption proceeds.", "The purpose of an exit load is to discourage investors from withdrawing their money from a mutual fund scheme too quickly.", "The exit load percentage and the period for which it is applicable vary from scheme to scheme.", "The exit load is typically higher for redemptions made within a short period of time, such as a few days or weeks, after investment.", "The exit load is clearly disclosed in the mutual fund scheme's offer document and key information memorandum."], "source_links": ["https://www.indmoney.com/mutual-funds/sbi-psu-direct-growth-3465", "https://www.indmoney.com/mutual-funds/aditya-birla-sun-life-psu-equity-fund-direct-growth-1005251"], "last_checked": "2026-04-27"}, {"type": "brokerage_fee", "scenario_name": "Brokerage Fee", "explanation_bullets": ["A brokerage fee is a charge levied by a broker or brokerage firm for facilitating a transaction", "This fee is typically a percentage of the transaction amount or a flat fee per trade", "Brokerage fees can vary depending on the type of investment, such as stocks, bonds, or mutual funds", "Some brokerage firms may offer discounted or waived fees for certain types of accounts or high-volume traders", "Brokerage fees are usually deducted from the investor's account at the time of the transaction", "Fees can impact investment returns, so it's essential to understand the fee structure before making a trade"], "source_links": ["https://www.indmoney.com/mutual-funds/icici-prudential-infrastructure-fund-direct-plan-growth-3333", "https://www.indmoney.com/mutual-funds/nippon-india-power-infra-fund-direct-plan-growth-3422"], "last_checked": "2026-04-27"}]
email_subject: Weekly INDMoney Voice of Customer Pulse
email_body: The weekly review pulse highlights significant user experience issues, financial and transactional concerns, and product and interface improvement needs. 40% of users face user experience issues, 30% encounter financial and transactional concerns, and 30% suggest product and interface improvements.

[{"type": "exit_load", "scenario_name": "Mutual Fund Exit Load", "explanation_bullets": ["A mutual fund exit load is a fee charged to investors when they redeem or withdraw their units from a mutual fund scheme.", "The exit load is typically expressed as a percentage of the redeemable value of the units.", "The purpose of an exit load is to discourage investors from withdrawing their money too quickly, allowing the fund manager to maintain a stable portfolio.", "The exit load percentage and the time period for which it applies vary across different mutual fund schemes.", "The exit load is usually highest for redemptions made immediately after investing and decreases or becomes zero as the investment tenure increases.", "The exit load is deducted from the redemption proceeds and the remaining amount is paid to the investor."], "source_links": ["https://www.indmoney.com/mutual-funds/sbi-psu-direct-growth-3465", "https://www.indmoney.com/mutual-funds/aditya-birla-sun-life-psu-equity-fund-direct-growth-1005251"], "last_checked": "2026-04-27"}, {"type": "brokerage_fee", "scenario_name": "Brokerage Fee", "explanation_bullets": ["A brokerage fee is a charge levied by a broker or brokerage firm for facilitating a transaction.", "It can be a flat fee or a percentage of the transaction amount.", "Brokerage fees are common in various financial markets, including stocks, bonds, and mutual funds.", "The fee is typically deducted from the investor's account at the time of the transaction.", "Brokerage fees can vary depending on the brokerage firm, type of transaction, and other factors.", "Investors should review and understand the brokerage fee structure before engaging in any transactions."], "source_links": ["https://www.indmoney.com/mutual-funds/icici-prudential-infrastructure-fund-direct-plan-growth-3333", "https://www.indmoney.com/mutual-funds/nippon-india-power-infra-fund-direct-plan-growth-3422"], "last_checked": "2026-04-27"}]
